body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

On OpenClaw 2026.3.23-2, Gemini-based memory embeddings fail behind a normal HTTP/HTTPS proxy, even though direct
  Gemini embedding requests succeed on the same machine.

  This affects both:

  - in-app memory_search
  - openclaw memory status/search/index

  The failure shows up as:

  {
    "results": [],
    "disabled": true,
    "unavailable": true,
    "error": "fetch failed",
    "warning": "Memory search is unavailable due to an embedding/provider error.",
    "action": "Check embedding provider configuration and retry memory_search."
  }

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure Gemini memory embeddings under agents.defaults.memorySearch
2. Run OpenClaw behind a standard outbound proxy
3. Run either:

openclaw memory status --agent main --deep --index

or:

openclaw memory search --agent main "test"

or trigger memory_search inside the app.

### Expected behavior

Memory indexing and search should work normally through the configured proxy, just like other outbound HTTP paths.


### Actual behavior

Memory embeddings fail with fetch failed.

Typical CLI output:

Embeddings: unavailable
Embeddings error: fetch failed

App/tool output:

{
  "results": [],
  "disabled": true,
  "unavailable": true,
  "error": "fetch failed"
}

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.23-2

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04

### Install method

_No response_

### Model

gemini/gemini-embedding-001

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw -> local proxies -> gemini

### Additional provider/model setup details

## Important debugging result

Direct Gemini embedding requests succeed on the same machine, with the same API key, through the same proxy.

Both of these work:

- embedContent
- batchEmbedContents

So this does not appear to be:

- a bad Gemini API key
- a Gemini endpoint outage
- a malformed embedding payload
- invalid memory file contents

It looks specific to the OpenClaw memory embedding HTTP path.

———

## Suspected root cause

In 2026.3.23-2, the memory embedding path still appears to use a strict guarded fetch path for remote memory HTTP
calls.

Relevant bundle section:

[dist/pi-embedded-CbCYZxIb.js]

async function withRemoteHttpResponse(params) {
  const { response, release } = await fetchWithSsrFGuard({
    url: params.url,
    init: params.init,
    policy: params.ssrfPolicy,
    auditContext: params.auditContext ?? "memory-remote"
  });
  try {
    return await params.onResponse(response);
  } finally {
    await release();
  }
}

From local testing, changing this memory path to use the env-proxy-aware guarded fetch helper fixes the issue.

For example, switching to:

fetchWithSsrFGuard(withTrustedEnvProxyGuardedFetchMode(...))

restores successful memory indexing and memory_search behind proxy.

So the issue seems to be that memory remote embedding requests do not honor env-proxy handling consistently, even
though other OpenClaw network paths already do.

———

## Suggested fix

Please make the memory remote embedding HTTP path proxy-aware in the same way as other OpenClaw outbound HTTP paths.

It should honor:

- HTTP_PROXY
- HTTPS_PROXY
- ALL_PROXY
- NO_PROXY
- NODE_USE_ENV_PROXY

or reuse the existing env-proxy-aware guarded fetch mode already used elsewhere in the codebase.

———

## Temporary workaround

A local patch to the memory remote HTTP helper fixes the issue.

After patching the memory fetch path to use the env-proxy-aware guarded fetch helper and restarting the gateway:

- openclaw memory status --agent main --deep shows Embeddings: ready
- openclaw memory search ... returns normal results
- in-app memory_search works again

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Closing this as implemented after Codex automated review.

Current main implements the reported Gemini memory embedding HTTP(S) proxy path. Gemini direct and batch memory embedding calls use the shared memory-host remote HTTP helper, and that helper now switches guarded fetch into trusted env-proxy mode when HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY applies while preserving strict SSRF pinning for NO_PROXY and ALL_PROXY-only cases. The changelog records this in 2026.4.25 (Unreleased) via #71506; no shipped release tag is identifiable yet.

Best possible solution:

Close this issue as implemented on main. Keep the shared memory-host SDK env-proxy guard as the canonical fix for Gemini memory embeddings; if maintainers want ALL_PROXY-only behavior later, track that separately as an explicit transport/security design change.

What I checked:

- **Issue report targets Gemini memory embeddings behind HTTP(S) proxy:** The issue body reports OpenClaw 2026.3.23-2 failing `memory_search` and `openclaw memory status/search/index` with Gemini embeddings behind a normal HTTP/HTTPS proxy, and suspects the memory remote HTTP helper stayed on strict guarded fetch.
- **Gemini memory embedding calls use the shared remote HTTP helper:** Gemini `embedContent` and `batchEmbedContents` requests call `withRemoteHttpResponse`, so the shared memory-host HTTP transport now covers the reported Gemini memory path. ([`extensions/google/embedding-provider.ts:183`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/be1d656514aa/extensions/google/embedding-provider.ts#L183), [be1d656514aa](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/be1d656514aa))
- **Gemini batch embedding HTTP calls use the same helper:** Async Gemini batch upload, create, status, and download requests also call `withRemoteHttpResponse`, covering the batch indexing path used by memory status/index. ([`extensions/google/embedding-batch.ts:104`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/be1d656514aa/extensions/google/embedding-batch.ts#L104), [be1d656514aa](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/be1d656514aa))
- **Memory-host helper now enables trusted env-proxy mode:** `withRemoteHttpResponse` calls `shouldUseEnvHttpProxyForUrl(params.url)` and adds `mode: GUARDED_FETCH_MODE.TRUSTED_ENV_PROXY` when that target should use the environment HTTP proxy. ([`src/memory-host-sdk/host/remote-http.ts:15`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/be1d656514aa/src/memory-host-sdk/host/remote-http.ts#L15), [be1d656514aa](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/be1d656514aa))
- **Proxy gate honors HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY and NO_PROXY:** The env-proxy gate resolves HTTP/HTTPS proxy variables for the target protocol and returns false when `NO_PROXY` matches, matching the safety behavior described in the issue's suggested fix while intentionally not auto-upgrading ALL_PROXY-only cases. ([`src/infra/net/proxy-env.ts:57`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/be1d656514aa/src/infra/net/proxy-env.ts#L57), [be1d656514aa](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/be1d656514aa))
- **Trusted env-proxy mode routes through EnvHttpProxyAgent:** `fetchWithSsrFGuard` creates an env HTTP proxy dispatcher when trusted env-proxy mode is active and `shouldUseEnvHttpProxyForUrl` says the current URL should use the proxy. ([`src/infra/net/fetch-guard.ts:357`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/be1d656514aa/src/infra/net/fetch-guard.ts#L357), [be1d656514aa](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/be1d656514aa))

So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.

Codex Review notes: model gpt-5.5, reasoning high; reviewed against [be1d656514aa](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/be1d656514aa485073287cea50e81e1d0e4163cb); fix evidence: commit [be1d656514aa](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/be1d656514aa485073287cea50e81e1d0e4163cb).

<!-- clawsweeper-review item=54279 -->

title: [Bug]: memory_search with Gemini embeddings does not honor HTTP(S) proxy in 2026.3.23-2
